title: dianthus alpinus var. subacaulis
intro: Dianthus alpinus var. subacaulis, also known as Alpine Pink or Alpine Sweet William, is a perennial plant that belongs to the carnation family. Traditionally, this plant has been used in herbal medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and astringent properties. The health benefits of Dianthus alpinus var. subacaulis include its ability to help soothe digestive issues, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory problems such as bronchitis and coughs. The plant can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and it has also been used in traditional folk remedies for its antiseptic and antifungal properties.
